input.prompt:
Camera DOLLY-IN / push-in shot. Begins at top-down overhead view of an open sketchbook on a wooden desk: a photograph taped to the left page, a hand-drawn pencil and watercolor portrait of a young boy on the right page. The camera slowly and smoothly pushes IN toward the illustrated portrait on the right page over the course of the clip.

[0.0 – 1.5s]: Begin push-in. The sketchbook is still mostly visible; the photograph on the left edge of frame, the right page with the illustration centered.

[1.5 – 3.5s]: Camera continues moving forward. The frame tightens — the photograph drifts off the left edge of frame, the desk and page edges leave the frame at the corners, the illustrated portrait grows larger and more central.

[3.5 – 5.0s]: Camera settles. The illustrated portrait now fills the entire frame as a standalone illustration on cream paper, matching the end frame exactly. The sketchbook context is no longer visible.

The illustrated portrait itself does not change — only the framing tightens via the dolly-in.

Constraints: smooth steady dolly-in only — no pan, no tilt, no jitter. NO visible pencil, NO visible hand, no other characters. Single continuous take. The clip MUST end exactly at the provided end frame for seamless stitching.
